summary: Gate exit code was 0, but Phase 1 verification is not production-safe: the main gate masks failures (no pipefail), pytest did not run, Langfuse is unreachable but treated as optional, PII canary and kill-9 checks are simulated, CRITICAL transition_deal is not fail-closed on missing approval, and checkpoint encryption is not integrated via a SecurePostgresSaver.

blockers.title: Phase1 gate is not fail-closed; pytest did not run (missing `pipefail` masks failures)
blockers.details: `scripts/gates/phase1_gate.sh` uses `set -e` only (no `set -o pipefail`) and runs critical checks through pipelines (`... | tee ... | tail ...`). In bash, the pipeline exit code is the last command, so failures in `python3 -m pytest` (and other python checks piped into `tee`) are ignored. Evidence: `gate_artifacts/phase1/pytest_output.log` contains `/usr/bin/python3: No module named pytest`, yet the gate reported `pytest: PASSED` and exited 0. This violates acceptance criteria: `pytest tests/` must pass and the gate must exit nonzero on any failure.
blockers.expected: Gate fails if pytest is missing or any required check fails; `pytest tests/` actually executes and must pass.
blockers.actual: Gate exits 0 and prints `PHASE1_GATE=PASSED` even when pytest is missing and other piped checks can fail silently.
blockers.recommended_fix: Make the gate fail-closed: use `set -euo pipefail` (or capture `PIPESTATUS`) and avoid pipelines that discard the primary command’s exit code; run pytest from the project environment (e.g., `./venv/bin/python -m pytest`); mark required checks as required.
validation_steps: Break a test intentionally and confirm `./scripts/gates/phase1_gate.sh` exits nonzero.
Run `./scripts/gates/phase1_gate.sh` in an env without pytest and confirm it fails with a clear error.
Run `./scripts/gates/phase1_gate.sh` after installing deps and confirm `pytest tests/` runs and passes.

blockers.title: CRITICAL `transition_deal` is not fail-closed on missing approval
blockers.details: `src/core/agent/deal_tools.py` logs a warning when `approval_id` is missing, but still proceeds to call `client.transition_deal(...)`. This violates Decision Lock / mission requirement that CRITICAL tools must only execute after HITL approval. The current behavior permits executing the Deal API transition without approval context if called incorrectly.
repro_steps: python3 scripts/gates/tests/test_deal_tools_execution.py  # calls transition_deal_tool with no approval_id
Inspect logs and/or Deal API server access logs for the call
blockers.expected: If `approval_id` is absent, `transition_deal_tool` should return a structured error and MUST NOT contact Deal API.
blockers.actual: `transition_deal_tool` warns but still calls Deal API transition endpoint.
blockers.recommended_fix: Enforce approval in the handler: if `approval_id` missing/invalid, return `success:false` with an error_code (e.g., `APPROVAL_REQUIRED`) and do not make HTTP calls; add a unit/integration test asserting no outbound call occurs without approval.
validation_steps: Run `python3 scripts/gates/tests/test_deal_tools_execution.py` and confirm transition_deal fails locally without outbound HTTP when approval is missing.
Create an approved context and confirm transition executes and returns success.

blockers.title: Checkpoint encryption not integrated with checkpoint persistence; `SecurePostgresSaver` missing and kill-9 is simulated
blockers.details: `src/core/security/checkpoint_encryption.py` claims it “Provides SecurePostgresSaver wrapper” but no such wrapper exists (repo search finds only the docstring). Encryption helpers are not referenced anywhere else in `src/`, so checkpoint persistence is not actually encrypted. Additionally, `scripts/gates/kill9_encrypted_test.sh` explicitly simulates kill-9 recovery rather than performing a real kill -9 + restart + DB-backed resume, and it also uses `python3 ... | tee ...` without pipefail, making failures hard to detect. This does not meet Phase 1 P0 encryption and kill-9 recovery requirements.
blockers.expected: A real `SecurePostgresSaver` wraps the actual checkpoint saver so checkpoint blobs/writes are encrypted at rest in Postgres; kill -9 recovery test demonstrates resume from encrypted persisted checkpoint.
blockers.actual: Only standalone encrypt/decrypt tests exist; no saver wrapper/integration; kill-9 test is simulation and can mask failures.
blockers.recommended_fix: Implement `SecurePostgresSaver` wrapping the real saver (encrypt on write, decrypt on read for the specified columns) and wire it into runtime; update kill-9 gate to run a real process crash/restart and verify DB ciphertext (`ENC:v1:`) plus correct resume; add `pipefail`/exit-code correctness to scripts.
validation_steps: Demonstrate a persisted checkpoint row contains ciphertext (no plaintext) and begins with `ENC:v1:`.
Run an actual kill -9 test that restarts the process and resumes from DB-backed checkpoint successfully.
Ensure the kill-9 gate fails when encryption is misconfigured or resume breaks.

blockers.title: Langfuse required by spec/acceptance but gate treats it as optional and passes while unreachable
blockers.details: `scripts/gates/phase1_gate.sh` runs the Langfuse health check as optional (`required=false`) and proceeds even when Langfuse is unreachable. The produced artifact confirms Langfuse is unreachable, and the soak artifact shows 60/60 Langfuse failures while still reporting PASS. This violates the Decision Lock and acceptance criteria requiring Langfuse at :3001 with a passing health check.
blockers.expected: Phase1 gate fails if `curl -f http://localhost:3001/api/public/health` fails; soak should not PASS while Langfuse is failing if Langfuse is required.
blockers.actual: Gate exits 0 with Langfuse unreachable and soak PASS despite `failures_by_service.langfuse = 60`.
blockers.recommended_fix: Make Langfuse gate required (or clearly gated by an explicit env opt-out); ensure compose brings up Langfuse or the gate asserts it is running; adjust soak verdict logic to treat Langfuse failure as failing when Langfuse is required.
validation_steps: Start Langfuse (`docker-compose up -d langfuse`) and confirm `curl -f http://localhost:3001/api/public/health` returns 200.
Stop Langfuse and confirm `./scripts/gates/phase1_gate.sh` exits nonzero.
Re-run soak and confirm Langfuse failures affect verdict when required.

blockers.title: PII canary gate does not actually inject canary and cannot verify DB/traces; report is misleading
blockers.details: `scripts/gates/pii_canary_gate.sh` explicitly performs a simulated injection (no agent invocation) and silently skips the DB check when `psql` is unavailable. Despite that, the gate can still PASS and the artifact report lists “PostgreSQL database” as scanned. This does not prove the canary is absent from logs/traces/DB as required for a stop-ship gate.
blockers.expected: Gate injects the canary through a real agent/tool invocation path, then deterministically scans logs, Langfuse traces, and the database; it FAILS if any scan cannot be performed.
blockers.actual: Injection is simulated; DB scan is skipped if tooling missing; gate still reports PASS and produces a report that implies DB was scanned.
blockers.recommended_fix: Implement real canary injection (invoke Agent API with canary in message/tool args); query DB via a deterministic method (python + psycopg/asyncpg) instead of requiring `psql`; export/scan Langfuse traces; fail closed if any required scan cannot run.
validation_steps: Run the canary gate and confirm it invokes the real agent execution path.
Confirm the report includes explicit DB query results and trace scan results.
Intentionally log the canary in a controlled test and confirm the gate FAILS.

blockers.title: Agent API port does not match Decision Lock (:8095); repo uses :8091
blockers.details: Decision Lock fixes Agent API at :8095, but this repo’s `docker-compose.yml` exposes `backend` on :8091 and the Phase 1 gate defaults `BACKEND_URL` to `http://localhost:8091`. There is no reference to :8095 in the repo. This violates the hard port constraint in the mission/acceptance.
blockers.expected: Agent API is reachable on port 8095 and all scripts/configs reflect that fixed port.
blockers.actual: Backend/gates target port 8091; port 8095 is absent from the repo.
blockers.recommended_fix: Align service port to :8095 (compose + runtime config + gates + docs) or clearly separate ‘backend’ vs ‘agent api’ services and ensure the agent API is implemented and bound to :8095 as locked.
validation_steps: Bring up services and confirm `curl -f http://localhost:8095/health/...` works.
Re-run `./scripts/gates/phase1_gate.sh` without overriding `BACKEND_URL` and confirm it targets :8095.

majors.title: Deal API endpoint paths do not match the locked contract (`/deals` vs `/api/deals`)
majors.details: Authoritative docs (e.g., ZakOps-Ultimate-Master-Document-v2.md §5.2) specify Deal API endpoints `/deals`, `/deals/{id}`, `/deals/{id}/transition` for the three Phase 1 tools. Implementation and artifacts use `/api/deals` (client + probe + gate tests). This is a spec mismatch that must be resolved by aligning code to the locked contract or implementing a compatibility layer.
majors.expected: Paths used by the tool wiring and probe match the locked contract endpoints.
majors.actual: Probe/client/gate artifacts use `/api/deals` while docstrings and roadmap specify `/deals`.
majors.recommended_fix: Pick the locked contract and make it consistent end-to-end (probe output, client paths, artifacts, and docs). If the existing Deal API only serves `/api/deals`, add a gateway/router to expose `/deals` as specified (or update the locked contract via the proper decision process).
validation_steps: Re-run the contract probe and confirm it reports the final agreed paths consistently.
Re-run the phase1 gate and confirm artifacts report the correct endpoints.

majors.title: Deal API contract probe output is internally inconsistent/misleading
majors.details: `scripts/probes/deal_api_contract_probe.py` calls `/api/deals` but prints and/or records endpoints as `/deals` in multiple places. The resulting `gate_artifacts/phase1/deal_api_contract.json` mixes `endpoint: /deals/{id}` with `required_endpoints.path: /api/deals/{id}`. This undermines the goal of using the probe to confirm endpoint shapes/paths before hardcoding.
majors.expected: Probe prints and writes consistent endpoint/method/path information reflecting what was actually called and what tools will use.
majors.actual: Probe calls `/api/*` but prints `/deals` and writes mixed endpoint fields in the contract JSON.
majors.recommended_fix: Normalize probe output: store and display the exact called paths; ensure `probes.*.endpoint` and `required_endpoints.*.path` are consistent and match the agreed contract; add assertions in a pytest/integration test to prevent regressions.
validation_steps: Run the probe and confirm stdout and JSON artifact agree on paths and methods.
Add/verify a test that fails if contract fields are inconsistent.

majors.title: Deal tools gate does not validate actual tool execution and passes even when Deal API calls fail
majors.details: `scripts/gates/tests/test_deal_tools.py` primarily verifies code structure via string matching and then calls the Deal API directly. It treats API call failures as warnings and still exits 0 with “PASS: Deal tools verified”, so the Phase 1 gate can pass without real tool wiring or a running Deal API.
majors.expected: Gate fails if Deal API is unreachable or if tool wiring cannot execute against the real API; the gate should exercise the tool handlers, not just direct HTTP calls and string checks.
majors.actual: Script logs WARN on API failures and still exits 0.
majors.recommended_fix: Replace/augment this gate test to import and execute the actual tool handlers (`list_deals_tool`, `get_deal_tool`, `transition_deal_tool`) against the real Deal API with strict pass/fail criteria; require artifacts to be produced and reflect real execution.
validation_steps: Stop Deal API and confirm the deal tools gate fails.
Start Deal API and confirm the gate executes the tool handlers and produces artifacts reflecting real tool execution.

majors.title: SafeLogger does not prevent raw content in log message strings; raw payload logging exists elsewhere
majors.details: `SafeLogger` only sanitizes `extra` kwargs; it cannot redact raw content embedded directly into the `msg` string (e.g., f-strings). Additionally, non-SafeLogger logging in the codebase can log raw event payloads (example: `src/api/shared/sse_broadcast.py` logs `message['data']` on JSON decode errors). This risks violating the Decision Lock “no raw content” policy.
majors.expected: No raw prompts/tool args/results (or other protected raw content) are ever logged in plaintext; policy should be enforced even if developers accidentally use f-strings.
majors.actual: SafeLogger cannot sanitize the log message string; at least one instance logs raw message payload directly.
majors.recommended_fix: Add enforcement: (1) ban f-string logging for protected paths (lint/test), (2) update logging wrappers/handlers to detect and block suspected raw content in `msg`, and (3) refactor known risky log statements to use SafeLogger with hashed fields only.
validation_steps: Run a canary injection through real paths and verify the canary is absent from logs/traces.
Add a test/lint rule that fails on `logger.*(f"` in protected modules.

minors.title: Deprecation warning in contract probe (`datetime.utcnow`)
minors.details: Gate output shows a DeprecationWarning for `datetime.utcnow()` usage in `scripts/probes/deal_api_contract_probe.py`. This is not a functional blocker but should be cleaned up to avoid future breakage.
minors.expected: No deprecated datetime APIs; use timezone-aware UTC timestamps.
minors.actual: Uses `datetime.utcnow()` and emits a deprecation warning.
minors.recommended_fix: Replace with `datetime.now(timezone.utc)` (or equivalent) and keep timestamps ISO8601 UTC.
validation_steps: Re-run the probe and confirm no deprecation warnings are emitted.

minors.title: Langfuse docker-compose uses default secret fallbacks
minors.details: `docker-compose.yml` sets defaults for `NEXTAUTH_SECRET` and `SALT` via `${...:-...}`. Even if intended for dev, Phase 1 security requirements emphasize env-based secrets and no default secrets in production mode; defaults increase risk of misconfiguration.
minors.expected: Secrets required via env in production; defaults should be dev-only and fail-closed when `ENVIRONMENT=production`.
minors.actual: Defaults are present in compose file.
minors.recommended_fix: Gate defaults behind development mode or require explicit env vars when `ENVIRONMENT` indicates production; document required secret env vars.
validation_steps: Set `ENVIRONMENT=production` and confirm startup fails fast unless required secrets are provided.

next_actions_for_builder.summary: Fix `phase1_gate.sh` (and sub-gates) to be fail-closed: add `pipefail`, remove pipelines that mask exit codes, and run pytest from the project environment so failures stop the gate.
how_to_validate: Run `./scripts/gates/phase1_gate.sh` and confirm it fails when pytest is missing or tests fail.
Intentionally break a test and confirm the gate exits nonzero.

next_actions_for_builder.summary: Enforce HITL approval for `transition_deal`: fail closed without `approval_id` and ensure the gate demonstrates a real approved transition call.
how_to_validate: Call `transition_deal_tool` without approval and confirm no outbound HTTP occurs.
Call with a valid approval context and confirm Deal API transition returns success (2xx) and artifact shows non-probe execution.

next_actions_for_builder.summary: Implement and wire `SecurePostgresSaver` so checkpoint data is encrypted at rest in Postgres; update kill-9 gate to perform a real crash/restart resume against DB.
how_to_validate: Verify DB-stored checkpoint blobs/writes are ciphertext (e.g., `ENC:v1:` prefix) and contain no plaintext.
Run a real kill -9 + restart and confirm resume completes correctly.

next_actions_for_builder.summary: Make Langfuse mandatory and verifiable: bring up :3001, require health check 200, and provide a real trace proof artifact (not a placeholder markdown).
how_to_validate: Start Langfuse and confirm `curl -f http://localhost:3001/api/public/health` returns 200.
Stop Langfuse and confirm the phase1 gate fails.

next_actions_for_builder.summary: Replace simulated PII canary with a real canary injection and deterministic scans of logs, traces, and DB; fail closed if any scan cannot run.
how_to_validate: Run canary gate and confirm it invokes the real agent/tool path and scans DB/traces.
Intentionally leak the canary in a controlled way and confirm the gate fails.

next_actions_for_builder.summary: Align ports/endpoints to Decision Lock: Agent API on :8095 and Deal API paths consistent with the locked contract; update probe/client/tests accordingly.
how_to_validate: Confirm Agent API responds on :8095 and gate defaults target it.
Re-run contract probe + deal tools gates and confirm endpoints match the agreed contract consistently.

evidence: gate_artifacts/phase1/pytest_output.log: `/usr/bin/python3: No module named pytest` while gate reported `pytest: PASSED`.
scripts/gates/phase1_gate.sh:109 uses a pipeline with `tee`/`tail` without `pipefail`, masking failures.
gate_artifacts/phase1/langfuse_health.json shows `healthy: false` but `scripts/gates/phase1_gate.sh:167` treats Langfuse as optional.
scripts/gates/pii_canary_gate.sh:125 explicitly states simulated injection; scripts/gates/pii_canary_gate.sh:85 skips DB check if `psql` missing.
gate_artifacts/phase1/deal_api_transition_deal.json shows `probe_mode: true` and `note: no actual transition`.
src/core/agent/deal_tools.py:291-306 proceeds to call Deal API transition even without `approval_id`.
`rg -n SecurePostgresSaver -S src` only matches `src/core/security/checkpoint_encryption.py` docstring; no wrapper/integration implemented.
docker-compose.yml:73 exposes backend on :8091; DECISION-LOCK-FILE.md Quick Reference locks Agent API to :8095.
